key_insight: The Large Cancer Assistant (LCA) is a model-agnostic orchestration framework that separates multimodal data routing from AI inference to make oncology decision support more scalable and flexible.
clinical_relevance: It could help hospitals plug different AI models into clinical workflows without redesigning the whole system, supporting more adaptable decision support in cancer care.
method: Formalized the system as a 7-tuple architecture with “algorithmic impermeability” to keep orchestration independent of model internals.
Used an Entry Theory based on geometric deep learning to standardize multimodal patient data.
Routed outputs through a Standardized Intermediate Payload (SIP) and a Cancer Switching Module to decouple AI from hospital IT systems.
evidence_signal: In a proof-of-concept evaluation, the framework showed negligible orchestration overhead, invariant routing during model swaps, 100% recall for targeted supplementary data requests under anomalies, and successful multi-protocol execution.
watch_item: The results come from a proof-of-concept orchestration test, so real-world clinical integration, robustness, and interoperability still need broader validation.
paper_title: The Large Cancer Assistant (LCA): A Model-Agnostic Orchestration Framework for Scalable Clinical Decision Support in Oncology
